tree.name: Keon Ellis
tree.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Cavaliers traded De'Andre Hunter to Kings and 2027 2nd round pick to Bulls. Kings traded Keon Ellis and Dennis Schroder to Cavaliers, Dario Saric and 2029 2nd round pick to Bulls. Bulls traded Emanuel Miller to Cavaliers.

assetsGivenUp.name: De'Andre Hunter
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Caris LeVert and Georges Niang to Hawks for De'Andre Hunter. Cleveland also received a trade exception.
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward acquired from Atlanta Hawks

assetsGivenUp.name: Caris LeVert
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Ricky Rubio, 2022 2nd round pick (HOU own, became Andrew Nembhard), 2023 1st round pick (CLE own, became Ben Sheppard), and 2027 2nd round pick (UTA own) to Pacers for Caris LeVert and 2022 2nd round pick (MIA own, became Luke Travers)
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard acquired from Indiana Pacers

assetsGivenUp.name: Ricky Rubio
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Taurean Prince and 2022 2nd round pick (WAS own, became Bryce McGowens) to Timberwolves for Ricky Rubio
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard acquired from Minnesota. Out with torn ACL, 1yr/$17.8M

assetsGivenUp.name: Taurean Prince
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Part of 4-team James Harden trade. Cavaliers sent Dante Exum, 2022 MIL 1st round pick, 2024 2nd round pick to Nets for Jarrett Allen and Taurean Prince
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward acquired from Brooklyn in Harden mega-trade

assetsGivenUp.name: Dante Exum
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Jordan Clarkson to Jazz for Dante Exum and two 2nd round picks
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard from Utah

assetsGivenUp.name: Jordan Clarkson
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Isaiah Thomas, Channing Frye, and 2018 CLE 1st round pick to Lakers for Jordan Clarkson and Larry Nance Jr.
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard from Los Angeles Lakers

assetsGivenUp.name: Isaiah Thomas
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Kyrie Irving to Celtics for Isaiah Thomas, Jae Crowder, Ante Zizic, and 2018 BKN 1st round pick
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard from Boston, part of Kyrie Irving trade

assetsGivenUp.name: Kyrie Irving
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted #1 overall with pick acquired from Clippers in Baron Davis trade
assetsGivenUp.note: 2016 NBA Champion. #1 pick came from LAC via Mo Williams/Jamario Moon trade (Feb 2011).

assetsGivenUp.name: 2011 1st Round Pick (#1) via LAC
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers trade Mo Williams and Jamario Moon to Clippers for Baron Davis and 2011 1st round pick (unprotected)

assetsGivenUp.name: Mo Williams
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE sends Damon Jones to MIL and Joe Smith to OKC; MIL sends Mo Williams to CLE and Desmond Mason to OKC; OKC sends Luke Ridnour and Adrian Griffin to MIL
assetsGivenUp.note: 2009 All-Star with Cleveland. Key piece flipped for the #1 pick that became Kyrie.

assetsGivenUp.name: Damon Jones
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed 4-year deal. Career journeyman who became a key domino — flipped for Mo Williams, who was flipped for the #1 pick.

assetsGivenUp.name: Joe Smith
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE sends Drew Gooden, Larry Hughes, Shannon Brown, Cedric Simmons to CHI and Donyell Marshall, Ira Newble to SEA; CHI sends Joe Smith, Ben Wallace, 2009 2nd to CLE and Adrian Griffin to SEA; SEA sends Wally Szczerbiak, Delonte West to CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Veteran big from the massive 2008 deadline deal. Sent to OKC 6 months later for Mo Williams.

assetsGivenUp.name: Drew Gooden
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: CLE sends Tony Battie, 2005 2nd, 2007 2nd to ORL for Drew Gooden, Anderson Varejao, Steven Hunter
assetsGivenUp.note: Power forward acquired alongside Varejao from Orlando.

assetsGivenUp.name: Tony Battie
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: CLE traded Ricky Davis, Chris Mihm, Michael Stewart, and 2005 2nd round pick (became Ryan Gomes) to BOS for Tony Battie, Eric Williams, Kedrick Brown
assetsGivenUp.note: Acquired in the Ricky Davis trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Ricky Davis
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE traded Chris Gatling to MIA; MIA traded Ricky Davis to CLE and Don MacLean and cash to TOR; TOR traded Brian Skinner to CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Flashy guard who briefly lit up Cleveland.

assetsGivenUp.name: Chris Gatling
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE traded Shawn Kemp to POR; MIA traded Chris Gatling, Clarence Weatherspoon, and 2001 1st round pick to CLE; POR traded Gary Grant to CLE and Brian Grant to MIA
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward acquired from Miami in 3-team Shawn Kemp trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Shawn Kemp
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE traded Terrell Brandon, Tyrone Hill, and 1998 1st round pick to MIL; SEA traded Shawn Kemp to CLE; MIL traded Vin Baker to SEA and Sherman Douglas to CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: All-Star power forward from Seattle. Traded to Portland 3 years later for Chris Gatling.

assetsGivenUp.name: Terrell Brandon
assetsGivenUp.note: Drafted 11th overall by Cleveland in 1991. 2x All-Star, sent to MIL in the Shawn Kemp mega-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Larry Hughes
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed 5-year, $70M deal to pair with LeBron. Never lived up to the contract but became a trade chip.

assetsGivenUp.name: Jamario Moon
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as free agent. Previously played for TOR and MIA. Salary filler in the Baron Davis trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Channing Frye
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Cavaliers sent Anderson Varejao and conditional 1st to Trail Blazers, Jared Cunningham and 2nd round pick to Magic for Channing Frye
assetsGivenUp.note: Stretch big from Orlando

assetsGivenUp.name: Anderson Varejao
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers sent Tony Battie, 2005 2nd round pick, and 2007 2nd round pick to Magic for Drew Gooden, Anderson Varejao, and Steven Hunter
assetsGivenUp.note: Center acquired from Orlando on draft night

assetsGivenUp.name: Tony Battie
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded Ricky Davis, Chris Mihm, Michael Stewart, and a 2nd round pick to Celtics for Tony Battie, Eric Williams, and Kedrick Brown
assetsGivenUp.note: Center from Boston Celtics

assetsGivenUp.name: Ricky Davis
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Cavaliers sent Chris Gatling to Heat, received Ricky Davis from Heat
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard from Miami Heat

assetsGivenUp.name: Chris Gatling
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward acquired from Miami in 3-team Shawn Kemp trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Shawn Kemp
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE traded Terrell Brandon, Tyrone Hill, and 1998 1st round pick to MIL; SEA traded Shawn Kemp to CLE; MIL traded Vin Baker to SEA and Sherman Douglas to CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: All-Star power forward from Seattle. Traded to Portland 3 years later for Chris Gatling.

assetsGivenUp.name: Terrell Brandon
assetsGivenUp.note: Drafted 11th overall by Cleveland in 1991. 2x All-Star, sent to MIL in the Shawn Kemp mega-trade.

assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: CLE traded Shawn Kemp to POR; MIA traded Chris Gatling, Clarence Weatherspoon, and 2001 1st round pick to CLE; POR traded Gary Grant to CLE and Brian Grant to MIA

assetsGivenUp.name: 2005 2nd Round Pick (#44) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cleveland's 2nd round pick, became #44 overall

assetsGivenUp.name: 2007 2nd Round Pick (#54) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cleveland's 2nd round pick, became #54 overall

assetsGivenUp.name: 2018 1st Round Pick (#25) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cavaliers' own first-round pick sent to Portland in Channing Frye trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2018 1st Round Pick (#25) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cavaliers' own first-round pick sent to Lakers in IT trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2022 1st Round Pick (#24) via MIL
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers traded George Hill and Sam Dekker to Bucks for Matthew Dellavedova, John Henson, and a protected 1st round pick (became unprotected 2022)
assetsGivenUp.note: Originally protected 2021 1st, changed to unprotected 2022

assetsGivenUp.name: George Hill
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Cavaliers sent Iman Shumpert to Kings, Jae Crowder and Derrick Rose to Jazz for George Hill (from SAC) and Rodney Hood (from UTA)
assetsGivenUp.note: Veteran guard from Sacramento Kings

assetsGivenUp.name: Iman Shumpert
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Cavaliers sent Dion Waiters to Thunder, received Iman Shumpert and J.R. Smith from Knicks
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard/wing from New York Knicks

assetsGivenUp.name: Dion Waiters
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard drafted 4th overall by Cleveland Cavaliers

assetsGivenUp.name: Jae Crowder
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Part of Kyrie Irving trade. Celtics sent Isaiah Thomas, Jae Crowder, Ante Zizic, and 2018 BKN 1st round pick to Cavaliers
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward from Boston, part of Kyrie Irving trade

assetsGivenUp.name: Kyrie Irving
assetsGivenUp.note: Point guard drafted 1st overall by Cleveland Cavaliers

assetsGivenUp.name: Derrick Rose
assetsGivenUp.note: Guard signed as free agent by Cleveland. 1yr/$2.1M

assetsGivenUp.name: Sam Dekker
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Cavaliers acquired Sam Dekker from Clippers for draft rights to Vladimir Veremeenko and cash
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward from LA Clippers

assetsGivenUp.name: Cash considerations
assetsGivenUp.note: Cash and draft rights to Vladimir Veremeenko sent to Clippers

assetsGivenUp.name: 2024 2nd Round Pick (#53) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cleveland's own second-round pick sent to Nets in Harden trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2022 2nd Round Pick (#40) via WAS
assetsGivenUp.note: Originally Washington's pick, sent to Minnesota in Rubio trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2023 1st Round Pick (#26) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cavaliers' own pick, lottery-protected, conveyed in 2023 as #26. Sent to IND in LeVert trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2022 2nd Round Pick (#31) via HOU
assetsGivenUp.note: Houston's pick, sent to IND in LeVert trade

assetsGivenUp.name: 2027 2nd Round Pick via UTA
assetsGivenUp.note: Utah's pick, sent to IND in LeVert trade

assetsGivenUp.name: Georges Niang
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward signed as free agent by Cleveland

assetsGivenUp.name: 2027 2nd Round Pick via CLE
assetsGivenUp.note: Cleveland's own second-round pick sent to Bulls in Schroder 3-team trade
